questions: The ResizeObserver fires continuously during the 300ms padding animation. Should we debounce the tooSmall state change (e.g., 100-150ms) to avoid rapid condensed/standard flipping during the animation, or is the single threshold crossing sufficient?

When unlocking the generations pane, the reverse transition happens: padding shrinks, container grows, tooSmall flips to false. Should the layout swap back to standard immediately when height crosses the threshold, or should there be hysteresis (e.g., require 50px more than MIN_STANDARD_HEIGHT to switch back) to prevent flickering near the boundary?

The condensed layout has a '← Back' button (line 241-248) that only shows when `!forceCondensed`. With the change, this button will show whenever `tooSmall` is true (including from pane lock). Is that the intended behavior, or should it also be hidden when the cause is pane lock?

success_criteria.criterion: Locking the generations pane does NOT cause an instant layout jump — the layout transitions to condensed only after the LayoutMainContent padding animation reduces available height below MIN_STANDARD_HEIGHT
Unlocking the generations pane transitions back to standard layout when the container height exceeds MIN_STANDARD_HEIGHT
forceCondensed (compact mode) still forces condensed layout immediately without animation
All existing tests pass without modification (or are updated if they test the changed behavior)
No layout animation libraries added — only CSS transitions
The layout swap includes an opacity/transform transition so it doesn't feel like a hard cut
No flickering or rapid toggling between condensed/standard during the padding animation
Standard layout grid-template-rows changes (timeline maximize/restore) also animate smoothly
Total lines changed is under ~20 (proportional to the scope of the fix)
Manual smoke test: lock pane, unlock pane, resize window, compact mode all behave correctly

assumptions: The LayoutMainContent 300ms padding animation is the primary mechanism that causes the VideoEditorShell container to shrink — removing the isGenerationsPaneLocked shortcut will let the ResizeObserver detect this naturally.

The ResizeObserver threshold crossing happens cleanly (once down, once up) without needing debouncing, since the padding animates monotonically in one direction. If testing reveals flickering, we'll add a small debounce or hysteresis.

The '← Back' button showing in condensed mode when caused by pane lock (not just forceCondensed) is acceptable — the existing code at line 297 already handles the 'Editor' expand button for this case.
No other consumers depend on the isGenerationsPaneLocked → condensed instant-switch behavior. The only file using this logic is VideoEditorShell.tsx.
